👀💵 **Taxes, texts, & crypto: Palantir’s $130M IRS takeover
**
Palantir's Executive Director Alex Karp’s mass surveillance and autonomous weapons software empire [is embedded](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68754) deep inside the IRS, hoovering up data on Americans, according to records cited by The Intercept.

🌏 The firm fuses dozens of federal datasets - tax returns, bank records, healthcare data, crypto wallets, even dark web traces - into a single, searchable system to reportedly investigate a broad range of financial crimes.

This is data gorging on an industrial scale, warns the outlet.

💵 Since 2018, the IRS Criminal Investigation unit has been feeding Palantir’s Lead and Case Analytics (LCA) platform with sprawling, sensitive information.

The LCA [runs on Palantir’s Gotham ](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/63540)and Foundry platforms, enabling “analysis of massive-scale data to find the needle in the haystack,” according to the contract documents.

Over $130 million has already been spent to build and expand this system designed to map human connections, from calls and texts to emails. It also uses IP analysis to spot suspects faster.

Engineers of Palantir — whose [latest manifesto](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68441?single) reads like a blueprint for a techno-fascist AI-powered security state — are working hand-in-hand with IRS agents to tighten the loop between surveillance and action.

Watchdogs like American Oversight warn that this isn’t a software contract - it’s a surveillance heist “with minimal transparency.”

full_text:
🇮🇷💪🏻**Iran’s economy survived US ‘maximum pressure’ before — It will again**

A US naval blockade won’t break Iran’s economy, argues researcher Esfandyar Batmanghelidj in a post on X.
Donald Trump tried maximum pressure before, he reminds us, and it didn’t work.

Iranian crude exports were driven to near zero in the second half of 2019 due to intensified sanctions following the JCPOA withdrawal.

🪖 Storage tanks filled up fast, output of oil and refined fuels was slashed by roughly half.

But the National Iranian Oil Company rotated production shutdowns across fields instead of permanently idling them — basically switching wells on and off to protect reservoir pressure and avoid long-term damage (per Kpler).

Then COVID hit on top of sanctions, with fractured supply chains and collapsed demand.

📈 And yet… production climbed back, and exports slowly recovered.

EIA data across 2011–2024 shows a clear pattern: sharp drops during sanction peaks, followed by full rebounds each time.

Batmanghelidj’s takeaway is that the “blockade strategy” never tilted negotiations with Iran in Trump’s favor — and it won’t this time.

👍** **[**Boost us**](https://t.me/boost/geopolitics_prime)** | **[**Chat**](https://t.me/+tltgWD-GUqw0OTAy)** | ****@geopolitics_prime**

🇺🇸🇮🇷 **US bunker-busters cannot touch Iran’s power core**

The sobering military reality for the US and Israel is that even if they try to assassinate Mojtaba Khamenei, the current supreme leader of Iran, just as they did his father,[ they lack the capacity](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/67216).

It is rumored that Khamenei is sheltering in a deeply fortified bunker 500 meters underground.

Iran has constructed extensive underground "missile cities" within mountains, buried in hard granite like Shirkuh rock. 

👉 These complexes store long-range ballistic missiles and are designed for survivability against airstrikes, with maximum depths possibly reaching 500 meters in mountainous areas per IRGC claims.

The US GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator can pierce about 60 meters of earth/soil or roughly 18 meters of reinforced concrete. This renders the deepest sites beyond conventional bunker-busters.

Whenever the Americans and Israelis struck the underground missile complexes in Iran, they superficially damaged some tunnels or collapsed the entryways.

💣 Just before the recent ceasefire came into effect, US intelligence concluded it had significantly overstated the impact of its air campaign—by at least 50%—on missile launchers and underground facilities. 

About half of Iran’s missile launchers reportedly remain operational, and thousands of one-way attack drones are still in its arsenal, despite weeks of US and Israeli strikes. 

As witnessed after the spate of assassinations carried out by the US and Israel, Iran’s leadership structure has proven resilient, with multiple successors for key roles, while the [US key objectives in Iran](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/65973) remain unmet.

🇺🇸❌🇮🇷 **Trump, unable to win Iran war, may distract with new military ops **

Trump does not like long wars, and also doesn’t not like to lose face, says Chinese-Canadian author and educator Xueqin Jiang.  
 
So, Trump may “pretend this war doesn't exist anymore,” switching attention to Cuba.  

💬 “The Pentagon is on a plan to actually invade Cuba at some point,” says the professor.

He speculates that Trump might also threaten Canada, or opt to send in special forces “to attack the cartels in Mexico.”

👎 Trump’s fan girl Leavitt insists “direct talks” are underway in Pakistan, with Trump’s allies Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner heading there.

🔍 Meanwhile, reality says otherwise.
